macro_summary: Tech and semiconductor markets remain under pressure following a 20%+ drawdown in the SOX index, while the Nasdaq-100 has held comparatively better (-3% from peak), suggesting rotation into broader mega-cap tech. The dominant near-term macro event is the FOMC meeting July 28-29 (hold consensus ~87-90%), immediately followed by the densest earnings week of Q2 season: GOOGL July 22, MSFT July 29, and AAPL July 30. AI infrastructure capex guidance from hyperscalers will determine whether the chip selloff represents a dislocation or a fundamental reset. Options strategies are broadly preferred across ideas given elevated implied volatility and binary event clustering.
screening_note: 6 total overnight screening runs analyzed. Minimum threshold for inclusion: 2 appearances. All 5 qualifying tickers cleared the bar; 3 achieved unanimous 6/6 consensus.
key_events: FOMC rate decision: July 28-29, 2026 (~87-90% hold probability)
MSFT fiscal Q4 earnings: July 29, 2026 AH (same day as FOMC)
SKHY Korean share circulation event: July 29, 2026 (potential dilution pressure)
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings: August 26, 2026 AH (~$91-94B revenue consensus)

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in all 6 of 6 overnight screens with unanimous high conviction — the most consistent signal of the cycle. The stock trades at ~$202 vs. a 68-analyst consensus target of ~$302 (~49% gap) at a forward P/E of ~22x, near a decade low, despite 85%+ YoY revenue growth — a striking valuation dislocation driven by sector-wide semi selloff rather than fundamental deterioration. With 5 weeks to the Aug 26 earnings catalyst and EPS revisions running 33 up vs. 3 down in the last 90 days, a defined-risk call spread (Sep expiry) is the preferred expression over stock given elevated IV.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings confirmed August 26, 2026 post-market; revenue consensus ~$91.8-93.5B, EPS ~$2.08-2.12
ideas.edge: ~$100/share gap to 68-analyst consensus target ($302 avg); forward P/E ~22x vs. 5-year average of 72x at 85%+ YoY revenue growth; EPS revisions 33 up / 3 down last 90 days
ideas.bull_case: Sector selloff proves temporary; Aug 26 print confirms AI infrastructure demand remains robust, compressing the ~49% gap to analyst targets and re-rating the multiple toward historical norms. Pre-earnings run in a 5-week window amplifies call spread payoff.
ideas.bear_case: Semi sector sentiment stays weak into print; any guidance softness or hyperscaler capex pullback signals from GOOGL/MSFT this week could further compress the multiple and invalidate the near-term setup before expiry.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 of 6 overnight runs, making it a unanimous consensus pick despite medium conviction weighting. The ADR broke below its $149 IPO price (July 10, 2026) to ~$147 amid sector panic, creating a post-IPO dislocation vs. structural fundamentals: SK Hynix is the global HBM market-share leader supplying NVIDIA, with HBM supply sold out through most of 2027 and management guiding a memory shortage persisting beyond 2030. Barclays initiated Overweight at IPO and the 2-analyst 12-month consensus target is $342.50 — implying ~122% upside from current levels. Stock position preferred over options near-term given nascent options liquidity on the new listing.
ideas.catalyst: July 29, 2026: New Korean-market shares enter circulation (potential dip/reset entry); SK Hynix Korea exchange Q2 earnings expected late July/early August
ideas.edge: ADR trading ~$147, below $149 IPO price; 12-month analyst consensus target $342.50 (~+122% upside); Barclays Overweight initiation citing 70%+ upside; HBM supply sold out through 2027
ideas.bull_case: IPO-price break proves a capitulation low; July 29 Korean share event clears overhang and late-July earnings confirm HBM shortage narrative, driving ADR recovery toward analyst targets as the only pure-play AI memory name on US exchanges.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO volatility remains high and the July 29 dilution event triggers further selling pressure; nascent US options market limits hedging flexibility, and a broader semi sector downturn could push the ADR well below IPO price before fundamentals reassert.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in all 6 of 6 overnight screens as the consensus macro hedge and directional swing vehicle into the most event-dense 10-day window of 2026. The July 22-30 period stacks GOOGL/TSLA earnings, INTC, FOMC decision, MSFT, and AAPL into a single catalyst corridor. With the NDX down only ~3% from peak vs. SOX down ~20%, QQQ offers exposure to the mega-cap recovery thesis without direct semiconductor concentration risk. A straddle or strangle captures the binary FOMC + earnings volatility; a call spread expresses directional recovery if AI capex guides hold firm.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 28-29, 2026 (~87-90% hold probability) immediately followed by AAPL earnings July 30 — dual macro/earnings binary in a 48-hour window; hyperscaler earnings begin July 22 with GOOGL/TSLA
ideas.edge: Semis sold off 4%+ last week creating mean-reversion setup; Fed hold consensus removes primary rate-shock headwind for Nasdaq-100 growth multiples; NDX relative outperformance vs. SOX suggests rotation already underway
ideas.bull_case: FOMC holds as expected, hyperscaler AI capex guides remain strong through GOOGL/MSFT prints, and AAPL beats — QQQ captures the broad mega-cap re-rating with a call spread locking in a favorable entry after the semi-driven pullback.
ideas.bear_case: A surprise Fed hawkish shift or a string of weak AI capex guides from hyperscalers this week could accelerate selling into the FOMC window, making the straddle/strangle the safer structure over a directional call play.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in 3 of 6 overnight screens with unanimous high conviction from those runs. The stock trades at ~$401 vs. BofA's $500 price target — a ~25% ($99) gap — and demonstrated relative strength during last week's semi selloff (up ~3% on July 15 while semis cratered), signaling active rotation into non-chip mega-cap tech. The July 29 earnings date is uniquely high-volatility as it collides with the FOMC rate decision in a single session. GOOGL's July 22 print arrives first and provides a direct AI capex setup signal for the MSFT trade. Defined-risk call spread into the print is the preferred structure.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q4 2026 earnings July 29, 2026 AH — same session as FOMC rate decision; Azure growth rate and Copilot monetization are primary swing factors
ideas.edge: BofA Buy with $500 target vs. ~$401 current price (~+25% / ~$99 gap); demonstrated sector rotation outperformance vs. semis; GOOGL print July 22 arrives as advance AI capex signal
ideas.bull_case: Azure growth accelerates above expectations and Copilot monetization ramp becomes quantifiable; FOMC holds as expected, removing rate overhang — stock re-rates toward the $500 BofA target with call spread multiplying the move.
ideas.bear_case: FOMC surprise or weak AI capex signals from GOOGL (July 22) ahead of the print dampen sentiment; any Azure deceleration from ~30% growth would disappoint the market and invalidate the analyst target gap thesis.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 3 of 6 overnight screens with unanimous high conviction from those runs, but presents a more nuanced setup than the other ideas. Price data is conflicted across runs (~$283 in some, ~$333 in others), suggesting volatility and positioning uncertainty; critically, at least two runs flag the stock as trading at or above the Street consensus analyst target (~$318), making this a two-sided trade rather than a clear directional one. Tariff cost guidance for the September quarter and gross margin trajectory are the binary swing factors for the July 30 print. Elevated pre-earnings IV makes premium-selling structures (iron condor, put spread) or defined-risk strangles the cleanest expressions.
ideas.catalyst: Earnings confirmed July 30, 2026 after market close; tariff cost commentary on September quarter guidance is the key unknown swing factor
ideas.edge: Options-implied move historically 4-6% around earnings; $100B buyback program provides downside cushion; Services revenue $30.98B growing 16.1% YoY at 32%+ margins — but stock may be at or above consensus targets, creating two-sided risk
ideas.bull_case: 8 consecutive EPS beats continue; Services margin expansion and buyback floor support the stock; tariff impact proves manageable and September quarter guidance is in-line, driving a post-earnings relief rally of 4-6%.
ideas.bear_case: Stock priced at or above Street consensus targets entering the print; Keybanc downgrade to $250 (July 14) and tariff cost uncertainty for September quarter could trigger a 5-8% selloff if gross margin guidance disappoints — a bear put spread or iron condor profits from this scenario.
